description: A White House designed news-headline graphic. The full background is a dramatic black-and-white photograph of President Trump shot from behind, his arm raised with a clenched fist as he addresses a large seated audience in a conference hall (recessed ceiling lights visible), giving a powerful, defiant silhouette. A small white White-House mansion logo with 'The WHITE HOUSE' sits at top center. Overlaid near the top on a white rounded card is a red-and-black 'NEW YORK POST' logo and the headline in black: 'Trump rolls out ‘America First’ counterterror strategy targeting cartels, jihadists and domestic extremists,' with the phrase 'targeting cartels, jihadists and domestic extremists' emphasized in yellow highlight. The tweet caption is simply 'America First.' The technique pairs a heroic monochrome power-pose photo with a cited tabloid headline to project strength and a tough-on-threats counterterror agenda; intent is to broadcast a strongman, security-focused image to supporters.
notable_text: NEW YORK POST: Trump rolls out 'America First' counterterror strategy targeting cartels, jihadists and domestic extremists; The WHITE HOUSE
